title: Review: Flutter/Android foreground service + seed restore
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — The START_STICKY issue (nie-a8ds.1) will generate recurring user reports about phantom notifications; everything else is correctable before user impact.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) Android lifecycle edge cases I cannot exercise without a physical device — e.g., what happens when the system kills and restarts the service while the user is mid-reconnect in Dart; (2) the jmap.rs Space/join error path change — I traced the call site but didn't read the HTTP dispatch layer to confirm the (String, Value) tuple is consumed correctly; (3) FOREGROUND_SERVICE_CONNECTED_DEVICE behavior on API < 34 vs. >= 34 — I assessed the manifest comment but didn't verify the Android docs. Would raise confidence if: device testing on Android 13 and 14 against a real relay showing the notification appears, reconnects work, and the notification disappears on explicit disconnect.
